description: Adversarial inputs for SymbolTable typed-API migration — P10 Sub-task 5.2
notes: All programs validated against the phase-10/sub-task-5.2-symboltable-typed-api branch JAR.
Positive programs verified to exit 0. Negative programs verified to exit 1 with expected error substring in stderr.
Main.kt processes topLevelVariables first, then functions, regardless of source order.
This matters for negative cases 17-18 which test function-vs-var name collision order.
OPEN QUESTION: inner var shadowing function self-name (e.g. func f() { int f = 5 }) is REJECTED.
The function's self-decl puts its name in the outer (module) scope BEFORE the inner scope is created.
So the function body's declare('f') finds 'f' in the ancestor scope and fails.
This is unambiguous in the implementation but the spec (§2.2 declare semantics) does not call it out explicitly.
Flagged as OPEN QUESTION #1 below — the team should confirm this is intentional and document it.

open_questions.title: Function self-name leaks into own body scope — shadowing rejected
open_questions.description: When func f() is declared, its name 'f' is placed in the MODULE scope (outer). The function body uses a CHILD scope. Inside the body, declaring 'int f = 5' fails because declare() walks ancestor scopes and finds 'f' in the module scope. The spec §2.2 says 'Shadowing is rejected — current behavior, preserved here' but does not explicitly enumerate the function-self-name case. The behavior is consistent with the current rule, but a reader of the spec might expect the function body to be exempt from its own name (i.e., allowing 'int f = 5' as a local var inside f). Confirmed behavior: REJECTED with 'Duplicate declaration: f'. Recommend adding a sentence to §2.2 or §5.2 PITFALL list explicitly stating: 'A function's self-declaration (placed in the outer scope) blocks any inner binding with the same name, including inside the function's own body.'

open_questions.title: Top-level var declared after function with same name reports error at function position
open_questions.description: Main.kt processes topLevelVariables first, then functions. If source order is 'func add() {} \n int add = 99', the VAR is processed first (declares 'add'), then the FUNC's self-decl fails and returns 'Duplicate top-level declaration: add'. The error is reported at the FUNCTION's source position (f.getSourcePosition()), not the VAR's position. This may produce confusing error messages. Confirmed behavior: the error always points to the function, regardless of source order. The spec does not address error position ordering for this case. Not a correctness bug (the error is surfaced), but potentially a UX issue for P11+ error messages.

positive_entries.name: pos-01-toplevel-int-var
positive_entries.rationale: Top-level int var via TypedVariableDeclarationAndAssignmentData. Exercises declare() with kind=Variable, isReadonly=false, type=IntType. Baseline positive.

positive_entries.name: pos-02-toplevel-dec-var
positive_entries.rationale: Top-level dec var. Exercises WaterfallType.fromSourceText('dec') -> DecType path in TypedVariableDeclarationAndAssignmentData.

positive_entries.name: pos-03-toplevel-bool-var
positive_entries.rationale: Top-level bool var. Exercises WaterfallType.fromSourceText('bool') -> BoolType path.

positive_entries.name: pos-04-toplevel-char-array-var
positive_entries.rationale: Top-level char[] var. Exercises WaterfallType.fromSourceText('char[]') -> ArrayType(CharType) path. Array types are a distinct branch in fromSourceText.

positive_entries.name: pos-05-empty-void-function
positive_entries.rationale: Empty function, no return type. Exercises FunctionImplementationData self-decl with WaterfallType.forReturnType(null) -> VoidType, isReadonly=true, kind=SymbolKind.Function(parameters=[]). Zero-parameter path.

positive_entries.name: pos-06-function-returns-int
positive_entries.rationale: Function returning int. Exercises WaterfallType.forReturnType('int') -> IntType self-decl. Confirms forReturnType adapter is used (not fromSourceText) per §5.2.

positive_entries.name: pos-07-function-returns-bool
positive_entries.rationale: Function returning bool. Exercises forReturnType('bool') -> BoolType path.

positive_entries.name: pos-08-function-returns-dec
positive_entries.rationale: Function returning dec with one dec parameter. Exercises arg-decl with kind=Argument, isReadonly=false.

positive_entries.name: pos-09-function-returns-array
positive_entries.rationale: Function returning int[]. Exercises WaterfallType.forReturnType('int[]') -> ArrayType(IntType). Tests the array branch of forReturnType.

positive_entries.name: pos-10-function-multi-param-mixed-types
positive_entries.rationale: Three parameters of distinct types. Exercises FunctionImplementationData.kt:47 per-arg loop with name-first/type-second ordering and three distinct WaterfallType branches. Adversarially targets PITFALL #8 parameter ordering inversion.

positive_entries.name: pos-11-function-array-param
positive_entries.rationale: Array-typed parameter. Exercises WaterfallType.fromSourceText('int[]') -> ArrayType(IntType) in the per-arg declare path.

positive_entries.name: pos-12-recursive-function
positive_entries.rationale: Recursive function. The self-decl (FunctionImplementationData.kt:35) places 'fib' in the outer scope before the child scope is created, making fib visible inside its own body. Verifies recursion self-decl interplay.

positive_entries.name: pos-13-function-calls-sibling
positive_entries.rationale: Function calling a sibling function declared before it. Verifies self-decl chain: helper declares itself, then compute declares itself and calls helper by name.

positive_entries.name: pos-14-function-forward-ref
positive_entries.rationale: Forward reference: main calls helper which is declared after main in source. Because verification is type-check-light in P10 (no call-site type checking), this compiles. Confirms the symbol table does not require declarations-before-use for call expressions.

positive_entries.name: pos-15-sibling-functions-same-param-name
positive_entries.rationale: Two functions each with a parameter named 'n'. Each function body uses its own child scope (symbolTable.enterScope()). The scopes are independent and not retained after verification, so the second function's 'n' does not conflict with the first's.

positive_entries.name: pos-16-same-var-in-sibling-if-else
positive_entries.rationale: Same variable name in mutually exclusive if/else branches. Each branch gets its own child scope via IfBlockData's symbolTable.enterScope() calls. Shadow scopes are independent. Targets differentBranchesDontConflict deeper variant from DuplicateInnerDeclarationTest.

positive_entries.name: pos-17-three-branch-if-elif-else-same-var
positive_entries.rationale: Three-branch if/elif/else all declaring the same local var 'x'. Extends the two-branch positive case to exercise all three scope-creation callsites in IfBlockData (×3 symbolTable.enterScope() calls from the migration table in §2.4).

positive_entries.name: pos-18-same-var-in-sibling-for-loops
positive_entries.rationale: Same var name 'x' in two sequential for loops. Each ForBlockData.verify creates an independent child scope. The second loop's scope is created fresh; the first loop's scope has gone out of use, so 'x' does not persist. Tests ForBlockData's enterScope() migration.

positive_entries.name: pos-19-const-and-imm-top-level
positive_entries.rationale: Both 'const' and 'imm' modifiers at top level. Exercises isImmutable() -> isReadonly=true path for both modifier keywords (the preserved §2.4 reality note about grammar still emitting const/imm). Both should declare successfully with distinct names.

positive_entries.name: pos-20-deeply-nested-for-if-while
positive_entries.rationale: Four scope levels: function -> for -> if -> while. Each level declares a distinct var (a, b, c). Exercises enterScope() at ForBlockData, IfBlockData, WhileBlockData call sites — all three non-function scope construction callsites from §2.4 migration table.

positive_entries.name: pos-21-function-mutable-param
positive_entries.rationale: Reassigning a function parameter. Per §5.2 diff: per-arg declare sets isReadonly=false. The VariableAssignmentData.verify checks info.isReadonly; for args this is false, so assignment is allowed. Confirms args are NOT readonly (unlike function names).

positive_entries.name: pos-22-untyped-var-declaration
positive_entries.rationale: Untyped (walrus-operator) variable declarations in a function body. Exercises UntypedVariableDeclarationAndAssignmentData.verify's new declare() path with kind=Variable.

positive_entries.name: pos-23-function-const-inner-var
positive_entries.rationale: const-modified inner var. Verifies isImmutable() returns true for const modifier in function body context, storing isReadonly=true in the SymbolInfo. Distinct var name 'total' confirms no conflict.

positive_entries.name: pos-24-function-accessing-module-level-var
positive_entries.rationale: Function body that assigns to a module-level mutable var. The module-level scope has 'counter' as mutable (isReadonly=false). VariableAssignmentData.verify looks it up via the parent chain and finds isReadonly=false — no error. Tests lookup through parent chain.

positive_entries.name: pos-25-function-with-no-params-no-return
positive_entries.rationale: Two zero-param void functions. Each self-declares as Function(parameters=[]) with forReturnType(null). Tests that two distinct function names coexist in the same module scope without conflict.

negative_entries.name: neg-01-duplicate-toplevel-var
negative_entries.rationale: Two top-level vars with the same name. TypedVariableDeclarationAndAssignmentData.verify declares the second 'x' into the same scope where the first already lives. DeclareResult.Failure -> 'Duplicate declaration: x'.

negative_entries.name: neg-02-duplicate-inner-var
negative_entries.rationale: Two typed vars with same name in function body. Both declared in the function's own child scope. Second declare() finds 'x' already in owned map -> Failure. Error message: 'Duplicate declaration: x'.

negative_entries.name: neg-03-inner-var-shadows-function-param
negative_entries.rationale: Inner typed var shadows a function parameter. The arg 'n' is declared in the function's child scope; a subsequent 'int n = 1' in the same scope hits the own-scope check first. Error: 'Duplicate declaration: n'.

negative_entries.name: neg-04-inner-var-shadows-toplevel-var
negative_entries.rationale: Inner var shadows a top-level module var. The module scope owns 'x'; the function body is a child scope. declare('x') finds 'x' via parent.lookupOwning('x') -> DeclareResult.Failure -> 'Duplicate declaration: x'.

negative_entries.name: neg-05-duplicate-function-names
negative_entries.rationale: Two functions with identical names. First function's self-decl succeeds. Second function's self-decl finds 'add' already in the module scope -> DeclareResult.Failure -> 'Duplicate top-level declaration: add'.

negative_entries.name: neg-06-function-same-name-as-toplevel-var-func-after
negative_entries.rationale: Function name collides with a top-level var. Main.kt processes topLevelVariables first: 'int x=5' is declared. Then func x() is verified: self-decl finds 'x' in scope -> 'Duplicate top-level declaration: x' at func position.

negative_entries.name: neg-07-duplicate-param-names
negative_entries.rationale: Duplicate parameter names in one function. First arg 'a' is declared into functionSymbolTable. Second arg 'a' tries to declare into the same scope and finds 'a' already owned -> 'Duplicate declaration: a'. Exercises the per-arg loop at FunctionImplementationData.kt:47.

negative_entries.name: neg-08-pitfall7-assign-to-function-name
negative_entries.rationale: PITFALL #7 headline: reassigning a function name. 'f' is stored in the module scope with isReadonly=true (per §5.2 self-decl migration). VariableAssignmentData.verify looks up 'f', finds isReadonly=true -> 'Cannot assign to immutable binding \'f\''.

negative_entries.name: neg-09-pitfall7-increment-function-name
negative_entries.rationale: PITFALL #7 increment variant. IncrementStatementData.verify looks up 'f', finds isReadonly=true -> 'Cannot increment immutable binding \'f\''.

negative_entries.name: neg-10-assign-to-const-inner-var
negative_entries.rationale: Assigning to a const inner var. isImmutable() returns true for 'const' modifier -> isReadonly=true in SymbolInfo. VariableAssignmentData.verify -> 'Cannot assign to immutable binding \'x\''.

negative_entries.name: neg-11-increment-const-inner-var
negative_entries.rationale: Incrementing an imm inner var. isImmutable() returns true for 'imm' modifier -> isReadonly=true. IncrementStatementData.verify -> 'Cannot increment immutable binding \'x\''.

negative_entries.name: neg-12-assign-to-const-toplevel-from-function
negative_entries.rationale: Assigning to a const top-level var from inside a function. The module scope stores MAX with isReadonly=true. lookup() from the function's child scope walks the parent chain, finds MAX in the module scope, checks readonlyShadow chain — isReadonly=true. 'Cannot assign to immutable binding \'MAX\''.

negative_entries.name: neg-13-increment-const-toplevel-from-function
negative_entries.rationale: Incrementing a const top-level var from inside a function. Same lookup path as neg-12 but via IncrementStatementData.verify -> 'Cannot increment immutable binding \'total\''.

negative_entries.name: neg-14-for-loop-body-shadows-outer-var
negative_entries.rationale: For-loop body var shadowing a var in the enclosing function scope. ForBlockData creates a child scope; the child's parent chain includes the function scope owning 'i'. declare('i') in the child finds 'i' via parent.lookupOwning -> Failure -> 'Duplicate declaration: i'.

negative_entries.name: neg-15-while-loop-body-shadows-outer-var
negative_entries.rationale: While-loop body var shadowing a var in the enclosing function scope. WhileBlockData creates a child scope. declare('n') in the child walks ancestors and finds 'n' in the function scope -> 'Duplicate declaration: n'.

negative_entries.name: neg-16-duplicate-var-in-for-loop-body
negative_entries.rationale: Two vars with same name inside a for-loop body scope (not ancestor conflict, same-scope conflict). Both declared in the for-loop's child scope. Second declare finds 'x' in owned map -> 'Duplicate declaration: x'.

negative_entries.name: neg-17-function-param-shadows-toplevel-var
negative_entries.rationale: Function parameter named same as top-level var. Per §2.4: args are declared in functionSymbolTable (child of module scope). parent.lookupOwning('counter') finds the module-scope 'counter' -> DeclareResult.Failure -> 'Duplicate declaration: counter'.

negative_entries.name: neg-18-func-then-toplevel-var-same-name
negative_entries.rationale: Function declared before a top-level var of the same name. Main.kt processes topLevelVariables first: 'int add=99' is declared. Then func add() self-decl finds 'add' already in scope -> 'Duplicate top-level declaration: add'. Error reported at function's source position (OQ-2).

negative_entries.name: neg-19-pitfall7-assign-to-function-name-from-own-body
negative_entries.rationale: PITFALL #7 variant: function assigns to its OWN name inside its own body. The function's self-decl places 'f' in the module scope with isReadonly=true. The function body is a child scope; lookup('f') walks up to the module scope, finds isReadonly=true -> 'Cannot assign to immutable binding \'f\''.

negative_entries.name: neg-20-pitfall7-increment-function-from-own-body
negative_entries.rationale: PITFALL #7 increment variant from inside own body. 'counter' is stored as readonly (isReadonly=true) in module scope. IncrementStatementData.verify looks up 'counter' from the child scope, finds isReadonly=true -> 'Cannot increment immutable binding \'counter\''.

negative_entries.name: neg-21-duplicate-untyped-var-walrus
negative_entries.rationale: Duplicate untyped (walrus-operator) var declarations. Exercises UntypedVariableDeclarationAndAssignmentData.verify's new declare() path. Second 'x :=' in same scope -> 'Duplicate declaration: x'.

negative_entries.name: neg-22-inner-var-shadows-own-function-name
negative_entries.rationale: OQ-1 test: inner var with same name as the enclosing function. The function self-decl places 'f' in the module scope. The function body is a child scope; declare('f') finds 'f' via parent.lookupOwning -> 'Duplicate declaration: f'. This is consistent with the anti-shadowing rule but is an implicit consequence not called out in §2.2.

negative_entries.name: neg-23-duplicate-param-mixed-types
negative_entries.rationale: Duplicate parameter names with different types. Type difference does not exempt from duplicate detection — the SymbolTable is name-keyed. Second declare('a') -> Failure -> 'Duplicate declaration: a'. Exercises that the type stored in SymbolInfo is irrelevant to the duplicate-detection logic.
